intent: Build a task manager web app with Next.js. Features: user registration and login (use NextAuth with credentials provider + Prisma + SQLite), create/read/update/delete tasks with title, description, status (TODO/IN_PROGRESS/DONE), due date. Tasks belong to a user (user isolation). Filter tasks by status. Sort by due date. Simple clean UI. Include seed data with at least 2 users and 5 tasks. Use App Router.

stories.title: New User Registers and Creates Their First Task
stories.narrative: Alice has never used the app before. She registers an account, creates her first task with a due date, and verifies it appears in her task list with the correct details.

steps.action: Register a new account with email 'alice@example.com' and a password
steps.verify: Registration succeeds and the user is either automatically logged in or can log in with the new credentials
steps.verify_in_browser: Registration form submits without errors, user sees the main app page (not the login page)

steps.action: Create a task with title 'Buy groceries', description 'Milk, eggs, bread', status TODO, and due date tomorrow
steps.verify: Task is created successfully and has an assigned ID, correct title, description, status, and due date
steps.verify_in_browser: A task creation form or modal is accessible, submits without errors, and the new task appears on the page

steps.action: View the task list
steps.verify: The list contains exactly the newly created task with title 'Buy groceries', status TODO, and the correct due date
steps.verify_in_browser: The task list page displays the task with its title, description, status badge, and due date clearly visible

stories.title: Task Lifecycle: Create, Update Status, Edit, Delete
stories.narrative: Bob is a returning user who logs in with seed data credentials. He creates a new task, progresses it through statuses (TODO → IN_PROGRESS → DONE), edits its title and description, then deletes it and confirms it's gone.

steps.action: Log in as an existing seed user (one of the pre-seeded accounts)
steps.verify: Login succeeds and the user sees their existing tasks from seed data
steps.verify_in_browser: Login form accepts credentials, redirects to main page showing the user's seeded tasks

steps.action: Create a task with title 'Prepare presentation', description 'Q1 review slides', status TODO, due date 3 days from now
steps.verify: Task is created with status TODO and correct fields
steps.verify_in_browser: New task appears in the list with a TODO status indicator

steps.action: Update the task created in step 2 — change its status to IN_PROGRESS
steps.verify: Task now has status IN_PROGRESS, all other fields unchanged
steps.verify_in_browser: The task's status badge updates to show IN_PROGRESS

steps.action: Update the same task — change its title to 'Prepare Q1 presentation' and description to 'Include revenue charts'
steps.verify: Task title and description are updated, status remains IN_PROGRESS
steps.verify_in_browser: The task list reflects the updated title and description

steps.action: Update the same task — change status to DONE
steps.verify: Task status is now DONE
steps.verify_in_browser: Status badge shows DONE

steps.action: Delete the task created in step 2
steps.verify: Task no longer exists — attempting to fetch it returns nothing or an error
steps.verify_in_browser: Task disappears from the list immediately after deletion

steps.action: View the task list again
steps.verify: The deleted task is not present; the original seed tasks are still there
steps.verify_in_browser: Task list shows seed data tasks but not the deleted one

stories.title: Data Isolation Between Two Users
stories.narrative: Two different users each create tasks. Each user should only see their own tasks and never see the other user's data. This verifies user isolation is enforced at the data layer.

steps.action: Log in as the first seed user
steps.verify: Login succeeds
steps.verify_in_browser: User sees the main app page

steps.action: Note all existing tasks visible to this user, then create a task with title 'User1 Secret Project' and description 'Confidential data for user 1 only'
steps.verify: Task is created and visible in user 1's task list
steps.verify_in_browser: Task appears in the list

steps.action: Log out of the first user's account
steps.verify: Session is ended, user is no longer authenticated
steps.verify_in_browser: User is redirected to login page or sees logged-out state

steps.action: Log in as the second seed user
steps.verify: Login succeeds with different user credentials
steps.verify_in_browser: User sees the main app page

steps.action: View the task list for the second user
steps.verify: The list does NOT contain 'User1 Secret Project' — only the second user's own tasks are visible
steps.verify_in_browser: Task list shows only tasks belonging to this user, no trace of user 1's tasks

steps.action: While logged in as user 2, attempt to access the task created by user 1 (using the task ID from step 2) — try to read it, update it, or delete it
steps.verify: All attempts are rejected — user 2 cannot read, modify, or delete user 1's task
steps.verify_in_browser: Any direct navigation to user 1's task shows an error or redirects away

stories.title: Data Persists Across Logout and Login
stories.narrative: A user creates a task, logs out completely, logs back in, and verifies the task is still there with all its details intact. This ensures data is stored in the database, not just in session/memory.

steps.action: Log in as a seed user
steps.verify: Login succeeds
steps.verify_in_browser: User is on the main page

steps.action: Create a task with title 'Persistence Test Task', description 'This should survive logout', status IN_PROGRESS, due date 7 days from now
steps.verify: Task is created with all specified fields and an ID is returned
steps.verify_in_browser: Task appears in the list with correct details

steps.action: Log out of the account completely
steps.verify: Session is terminated, authentication token/cookie is cleared
steps.verify_in_browser: User is on the login/home page, no longer sees protected content

steps.action: Log back in with the same user credentials
steps.verify: Login succeeds and a new session is established
steps.verify_in_browser: User is back on the main page

steps.action: View the task list
steps.verify: 'Persistence Test Task' is present with title, description 'This should survive logout', status IN_PROGRESS, and the correct due date — all fields match exactly what was created
steps.verify_in_browser: The task is visible in the list with all its original details intact

stories.title: Unauthenticated Visitor Cannot Access Tasks
stories.narrative: A visitor who is not logged in attempts to view tasks, create a task, and access the task management pages directly via URL. All attempts should be blocked with appropriate redirects or error messages.

steps.action: Without logging in, try to access the main tasks page directly
steps.verify: Access is denied — the visitor is redirected to a login page or sees an unauthorized message
steps.verify_in_browser: The browser shows a login page or an access denied message, not the task list

steps.action: Without logging in, try to create a new task
steps.verify: The request is rejected — no task is created
steps.verify_in_browser: The task creation page or form is not accessible, or submission is blocked

steps.action: Without logging in, try to access a specific task by guessing a task ID in the URL
steps.verify: Access is denied — the task details are not exposed
steps.verify_in_browser: The visitor is redirected to login or sees an error, not the task content

steps.action: Without logging in, try to delete or update an existing task by its ID
steps.verify: The request is rejected — the task is not modified or deleted
steps.verify_in_browser: No changes are reflected even if the visitor crafts a direct request

stories.title: Filter Tasks by Status and Sort by Due Date
stories.narrative: A returning user with multiple tasks in different statuses and due dates uses the filter and sort features. They verify that filtering by each status shows the correct subset, and sorting by due date orders tasks correctly.

steps.action: Log in as a seed user
steps.verify: Login succeeds
steps.verify_in_browser: User is on the main page

steps.action: Create three tasks: (1) 'Task A' with status TODO and due date 5 days from now, (2) 'Task B' with status IN_PROGRESS and due date 2 days from now, (3) 'Task C' with status DONE and due date 10 days from now
steps.verify: All three tasks are created with distinct statuses and due dates
steps.verify_in_browser: All three tasks appear in the task list

steps.action: Filter the task list to show only TODO tasks
steps.verify: Only tasks with status TODO appear (including 'Task A' and any seed TODO tasks), tasks with IN_PROGRESS or DONE are hidden
steps.verify_in_browser: The filter control is visible and functional, list updates to show only TODO tasks

steps.action: Filter the task list to show only IN_PROGRESS tasks
steps.verify: Only IN_PROGRESS tasks appear (including 'Task B'), TODO and DONE tasks are hidden
steps.verify_in_browser: List updates correctly when switching the filter

steps.action: Filter the task list to show only DONE tasks
steps.verify: Only DONE tasks appear (including 'Task C')
steps.verify_in_browser: List shows only completed tasks

steps.action: Clear the filter to show all tasks, then sort by due date ascending
steps.verify: Tasks are ordered by due date from earliest to latest — 'Task B' (2 days) appears before 'Task A' (5 days) which appears before 'Task C' (10 days)
steps.verify_in_browser: The sort control is visible and functional, task order updates to reflect due date ordering

stories.title: Edge Cases: Empty Fields, Long Strings, Special Characters
stories.narrative: A returning user tests the boundaries of the app by trying to create tasks with empty titles, extremely long descriptions, special characters and emoji in titles, and HTML tags in descriptions. The app should handle all cases gracefully.

steps.action: Log in as a seed user
steps.verify: Login succeeds
steps.verify_in_browser: User is on the main page

steps.action: Try to create a task with an empty title (no title at all) but a valid description
steps.verify: The task is either rejected with a validation error, or if accepted, the task list handles it gracefully without breaking
steps.verify_in_browser: A validation message appears, or the form prevents submission of an empty title

steps.action: Create a task with a title containing emoji and unicode: '📋 Tâche spéciale — 任务 #1' and description 'Ñoño → résumé'
steps.verify: Task is created and all special characters are preserved exactly in the stored data
steps.verify_in_browser: The task displays with all emoji and unicode characters rendered correctly, no mojibake or encoding errors

steps.action: Create a task with a very long description (over 1000 characters of text)
steps.verify: Task is either created successfully with the full description preserved, or rejected with a clear length validation error
steps.verify_in_browser: If accepted, the long description is displayed without breaking the layout. If rejected, a clear error message is shown

steps.action: Create a task with HTML tags in the title: ' My Task' and description ' Description'
steps.verify: Task is created but HTML tags are escaped or stripped — no raw HTML is rendered or executed
steps.verify_in_browser: The title displays the literal text of the HTML tags (escaped), no script execution or image loading occurs — check browser console for XSS

steps.action: Rapidly submit the same task creation form twice (double-click submit)
steps.verify: Either only one task is created (idempotent), or two tasks are created but the app doesn't crash or show errors
steps.verify_in_browser: The UI handles the double submission gracefully — no error modals, no blank screens
